meta.summary: OpenAI introduced a new $100/month ChatGPT Pro tier, creating a mid-market price point between Plus at $20 and the existing $200 plan while offering 5x more Codex usage for heavier coding workflows. ByteDance Seed simultaneously surfaced multiple model artifacts — Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM — signaling continued competition in multimodal and speech-native systems. On the research side, the DMax paper proposed a new decoding method for diffusion language models aimed at much higher parallelism without the usual quality collapse, a potentially important efficiency lever if results hold up.

source_sections.label: Research Papers
source_sections.description: Research papers and paper-trending signals tied to the current day.

items.title: DMax: Aggressive Parallel Decoding for dLLMs
items.summary_short: This paper introduces DMax, a decoding method for diffusion language models. It aims to reduce error accumulation during parallel decoding so dLLMs can decode more aggressively without large quality losses

topic_refs.headline: DMax Paper Proposes Aggressive Parallel Decoding for Diffusion Language Models

items.title: The Lifecycle of the Spectral Edge: From Gradient Learning to Weight-Decay Compression
items.summary_short: Broadly important today: Dissects the spectral edge during grokking into gradient and weight-decay components, showing a transition from functional direction to compression axis

topic_refs.headline: Spectral Edge Dynamics Reveal Functional Modes of Learning

items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Dissects the spectral edge during grokking into gradient and weight-decay components, showing a transition from functional direction to compression axis.

items.title: Spectral Edge Dynamics Reveal Functional Modes of Learning
items.summary_short: This paper analyzes the spectral edge of parameter updates to identify low-dimensional functional modes of learning. It applies the method to grokking and argues that these modes are not captured by standard interpretability tools

topic_refs.headline: Spectral Edge Dynamics Reveal Functional Modes of Learning

items.spotlight_comment: Broadly important today: Uses spectral-edge analysis of parameter updates to reveal low-dimensional functional modes of learning during grokking that standard interpretability tools miss.

items.title: Sensitivity-Positional Co-Localization in GQA Transformers
items.summary_short: We investigate a fundamental structural question in Grouped Query Attention (GQA) transformers: do the layers most sensitive to task correctness coincide with the layers where positional encoding adaptation has the greatest leverage?

topic_refs.headline: Sensitivity-Positional Co-Localization in GQA Transformers

items.title: Dead Weights, Live Signals: Feedforward Graphs of Frozen Language Models
items.summary_short: We present a feedforward graph architecture in which heterogeneous frozen large language models serve as computational nodes, communicating through a shared continuous latent space via learned linear projections.

topic_refs.headline: Dead Weights, Live Signals: Feedforward Graphs of Frozen Language Models

items.title: Exponential quantum advantage in processing massive classical data
items.summary_short: This paper claims exponential quantum advantage for processing large-scale classical data. It presents explicit theoretical results supporting that advantage

topic_refs.headline: Exponential quantum advantage in processing massive classical data

items.spotlight_comment: This is unusually broad and paradigm-claiming work with explicit exponential-advantage results, making it important for the daily hotspot feed.

items.title: SkillClaw: Let Skills Evolve Collectively with Agentic Evolver
items.summary_short: Large language model (LLM) agents such as OpenClaw rely on reusable skills to perform complex tasks, yet these skills remain largely static after deployment.
items.spotlight_comment: Large language model (LLM) agents such as OpenClaw rely on reusable skills to perform complex tasks, yet these skills remain largely static after deployment.

items.title: When Numbers Speak: Aligning Textual Numerals and Visual Instances in Text-to-Video Diffusion Models
items.summary_short: Text-to-video diffusion models have enabled open-ended video synthesis, but often struggle with generating the correct number of objects specified in a prompt. We introduce NUMINA , a training-free identify-then-guide framework for improved numerical alignment.
items.spotlight_comment: Text-to-video diffusion models have enabled open-ended video synthesis, but often struggle with generating the correct number of objects specified in a prompt.

items.title: MegaStyle: Constructing Diverse and Scalable Style Dataset via Consistent Text-to-Image Style Mapping
items.summary_short: In this paper, we introduce MegaStyle, a novel and scalable data curation pipeline that constructs an intra-style consistent, inter-style diverse and high-quality style dataset.
items.spotlight_comment: In this paper, we introduce MegaStyle, a novel and scalable data curation pipeline that constructs an intra-style consistent, inter-style diverse and high-quality style dataset.

items.title: KnowU-Bench: Towards Interactive, Proactive, and Personalized Mobile Agent Evaluation
items.summary_short: Personalized mobile agents that infer user preferences and calibrate proactive assistance hold great promise as everyday digital assistants, yet existing benchmarks fail to capture what this requires.
items.spotlight_comment: Personalized mobile agents that infer user preferences and calibrate proactive assistance hold great promise as everyday digital assistants, yet existing benchmarks fail to capture what this requires.

items.title: Externalization in LLM Agents: A Unified Review of Memory, Skills, Protocols and Harness Engineering
items.summary_short: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly built less by changing model weights than by reorganizing the runtime around them.
items.spotlight_comment: Large language model (LLM) agents are increasingly built less by changing model weights than by reorganizing the runtime around them.

items.title: ClawBench: Can AI Agents Complete Everyday Online Tasks?
items.summary_short: AI agents may be able to automate your inbox, but can they automate other routine aspects of your life? Everyday online tasks offer a realistic yet unsolved testbed for evaluating the next generation of AI agents.
items.spotlight_comment: AI agents may be able to automate your inbox, but can they automate other routine aspects of your life? Everyday online tasks offer a realistic yet unsolved testbed for evaluating the next generation of AI agents.

featured_topics.headline: ByteDance Seed Releases Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview and Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM
featured_topics.summary_short: ByteDance Seed officially launched Seed 2.0. The announcement marks a major release in the company's core model family
featured_topics.why_it_matters: ByteDance is signaling active model iteration in both general-purpose and speech-native AI, relevant for anyone tracking Chinese frontier model competition and voice interface productization.
key_takeaways: ByteDance Seed announced Dola-Seed-2.0-Preview on Arena, indicating a public-facing preview model intended for evaluation or side-by-side comparison.
The company also introduced Seed Full-Duplex Speech LLM, emphasizing attentive listening, interference suppression, and more natural turn-taking in voice interaction.
The cluster points to simultaneous investment in text/model quality and real-time speech systems, a combination increasingly important for assistants, customer support, and device-native AI.

featured_topics.headline: OpenAI Launches ChatGPT Pro at $100/Month With 5x More Codex Usage
featured_topics.summary_short: OpenAI announced a new $100 per month ChatGPT Pro subscription tier. The plan reportedly includes five times more Codex usage than the $20 per month Plus plan
featured_topics.why_it_matters: OpenAI is refining monetization and segmentation for power users, which matters for enterprise seat expansion, developer retention, and pricing benchmarks across the AI application market.
key_takeaways: The new ChatGPT Pro plan is priced at $100 per month, filling the gap between OpenAI's $20 Plus tier and its $200 higher-end offering.
OpenAI says the $100 tier includes 5x more usage of Codex than ChatGPT Plus, positioning it for longer and more intensive coding sessions.
The move suggests OpenAI sees enough demand from prosumers and developers to support a materially higher ARPU tier without forcing users directly to $200 pricing.

featured_topics.headline: DMax Paper Proposes Aggressive Parallel Decoding for Diffusion Language Models
featured_topics.summary_short: This paper introduces DMax, a decoding method for diffusion language models. It aims to reduce error accumulation during parallel decoding so dLLMs can decode more aggressively without large quality losses
featured_topics.why_it_matters: If replicated, DMax could improve inference efficiency for diffusion language models and reopen the case for dLLMs as a practical alternative to autoregressive systems.
key_takeaways: The paper introduces DMax as a decoding paradigm designed to reduce error accumulation during parallel generation in diffusion language models.
According to the authors, DMax enables more aggressive decoding parallelism while preserving generation quality, targeting one of the core bottlenecks in dLLM deployment.
The work was surfaced via both Hugging Face Trending Papers and arXiv tracking, but the current evidence is still paper-level rather than production validation.

evidence.title: DMax: Aggressive Parallel Decoding for dLLMs
DMax: Aggressive Parallel Decoding for dLLMs

x_buzz.title: Is Anthropic limiting the release of Mythos to protect the internet — or Anthropic?
x_buzz.summary: Anthropic said this week that it limited the release of its newest model, dubbed Mythos, because it is too capable of finding security exploits in software relied upon by users around the world. Are real cybersecurity concerns a cover for a bigger problem at the frontier lab?
